body.u: Looking back over the past 70 years I feel that I have been quite successful. When I was at school I stayed on for one year and improved my qualifications. I got a job in a bank as an accountant I found that this job interested me. My only complaint is about the examination system in my school, I think that everybody should do C.S.E.s and O levels. I think this because all the people that just doing O levels have nothing to fall back on, but if they did C.S.E.s as well they would have something to fall back on. My job in a bank was a steady one but I always wanted to play in a group as a lead guitarist. I always thought about playing in a group as another way of getting things of your system. There was 3 boys I was going to form a group with but it never happened. All the time I was working in a bank I lived with my mum and dad. I never got married because you I suppose I didn’t meet the right sort of person. As I got older I still listened to the same music I liked when I was 16, I liked The Damned, Sex Pistols, Jam Clash, Buzzcocks and Sham 68. I also used to listen to the Beatles because I liked them very much. When I got to about 50 I used to go and watch cricket a lot, Kent still remained my favourite team. The batsman I used to enjoy watching had retired he was Vir Richards. I don’t watch football as much as I used to because the game had got too defensive. When I retired at the age of 65 I bought a house in a quiet part of minster and I settled down to laze away the years I had left. When I was young I played football and cricket but now I’ve slowed the games down to snooker, cards and Darts, at which I am pub (highlanders) champion. I used to play dominoes but I gave it up.
